steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks specifically for Wright's finding as presented in the text. The passage states: "Patricia C. Wright found [lunar intensity] to be positively associated with foraging by three-striped night monkeys." The correct answer must restate this observation — not the explanation for it, and not Brown's separate finding about pocket mice.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'During periods of high lunar intensity, three-striped night monkeys increase their reliance on vision.': This is the OFT explanation offered in the last sentence, not Wright's finding. Wright's study measured foraging behavior, not visual reliance. ✗
  • Choice B — 'As lunar intensity increases, Arizona pocket mice reduce their foraging activity.': This describes Brown and colleagues' finding (negative association in pocket mice), not Wright's. Wrong researcher, wrong species. ✗
  • Choice C — 'As lunar intensity increases, three-striped night monkeys increase their foraging activity.': Directly restates the passage: lunar intensity positively associated with foraging by three-striped night monkeys. Correct species, correct direction, correct researcher. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Increased lunar intensity is more beneficial for three-striped night monkeys than it is for Arizona pocket mice.': This is the comparative conclusion drawn from OFT to explain the discrepancy — not Wright's own finding. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
